---
title: "Wright map"
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Wright map}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    collapse: true
    comment: '#>'
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
 library(cgrasch)
```

## What is a wright map

A Wright map, named after one of the greats of Rasch theory, Geoffrey Wright, is a very common visualisation for Rasch models. It maps the person abilities against the item difficulties. It is very useful for assessing the degree to which the items on a test form are targeted to the candidates.
